user_query:
Please read input/repo_overview.md, input/constraints.yaml, and input/acceptance_template.md. I want to kick off an automated, multi-agent feature development workflow to add two production-ready endpoints to our service: a public GET /healthz with lightweight checks and rate limiting, and a protected GET /metrics that requires RBAC and is safe for scraping. Based on the inputs, produce these files:

1) output/task.txt — a single, copy-pastable task string for the workflow that clearly states: what to build; the key technical details and constraints pulled from the input files; acceptance criteria written as a checkbox list; success metrics; and a cautious rollout plan. Keep this self-contained.

2) output/stories.json — a JSON array of 3–6 well-scoped user stories that can be executed in parallel where possible. Each story must include: title, description, dependencies (list of other story titles or empty), and acceptance_criteria (array of concrete items).

3) output/runbook.md — a practical runbook that explains how to: start the workflow using the task string; monitor workflow status; resume from the last failed step; view recent logs; and optionally start/stop a simple dashboard. Also include the expected pipeline stages in order and describe what completion means for each stage. Use only relative workspace paths when you reference files under input/ or output/, and do not mention any specific product or tool brand names. Add a short tip on how to force-trigger agents if their polling interval causes delays.

4) output/acceptance_checklist.md — a reader-friendly acceptance checklist mirroring the acceptance criteria with at least 8 items, written as checkboxes.

Make sure the task string and stories reflect the service’s tech stack, constraints, and any security/compliance notes in the inputs. Write all files exactly to the specified output/ paths.
